entities.title: Platform Operations
entities.summary: Lambda deployment lifecycle, runtime operations, and secrets/configuration.

entities.title: PR Integration
entities.summary: Ingress and context assembly for GitHub pull request events.

entities.title: Review Intelligence
entities.summary: AI and deterministic review analysis with result publication.

entities.title: GitHub Webhook Ingress
entities.summary: Verifies webhook signatures, validates event type, and starts review workflow routing.

entities.title: Lambda Runtime Operations
entities.summary: Manages Lambda packaging/deployment lifecycle and operational health guardrails.

entities.title: Policy Checks
entities.summary: Runs deterministic checks for secrets, risky IaC changes, and repository policy constraints.

entities.title: PR Context Assembly
entities.summary: Fetches changed files and diff metadata and prepares bounded review context payloads.

entities.title: Review Orchestration
entities.summary: Calls Bedrock models, merges AI and deterministic findings, and handles graceful degradation paths.

entities.title: Review Publication
entities.summary: Publishes check-run summaries and inline comments back to GitHub pull requests.

entities.title: Secrets and Configuration
entities.summary: Manages GitHub App credentials, Bedrock config, and redaction-safe runtime configuration.

entities.title: REQ-PRR-001
entities.summary: While normal mode is active, when pull request synchronize event is received and webhook signature is valid, the pr review system shall verify webhook signature before processing review workflow.

entities.title: REQ-PRR-002
entities.summary: While pull request review is active, when pull request opened event is received, the pr review system shall construct diff context from changed files for developer review execution.

entities.title: REQ-PRR-003
entities.summary: Where ai review is enabled, when diff context is prepared, the pr review system shall request bedrock runtime analysis for review findings.

entities.title: REQ-PRR-004
entities.summary: Where policy checks are enabled, when pull request review is active, the pr review system shall evaluate deterministic policy checks for secret exposure plus infrastructure risk plus dependency changes.

entities.title: REQ-PRR-005
entities.summary: While pull request review is active, when review findings are generated, the pr review system shall publish check run summary to repository maintainer.

entities.title: REQ-PRR-006
entities.summary: If bedrock runtime is unavailable, then the pr review system shall publish deterministic policy-only review outcome.

entities.title: REQ-PRR-007
entities.summary: Where audit logging is enabled, when review findings are published, the pr review system shall persist redacted audit metadata for security engineer audit traceability.

entities.title: REQ-PRR-008
entities.summary: If review request rate limit is exceeded, then the pr review system shall defer processing.

entities.title: REQ-PRR-009
entities.summary: While pull request review is active, when review findings are generated, the pr review system shall publish inline comments with result metadata to repository maintainer.

entities.title: REQ-PRR-010
entities.summary: If review request rate limit is exceeded, then the pr review system shall notify platform operator.

entities.title: Separate webhook ingress from review orchestration
entities.summary:
Model webhook ingress, review orchestration, policy checks, and publication as separate
functional units with explicit interface and flow relationships.

fields.context:
GitHub webhook handling has different trust-boundary and failure-mode concerns than AI review
orchestration and publication back to GitHub.
fields.decision:
Model webhook ingress, review orchestration, policy checks, and publication as separate
functional units with explicit interface and flow relationships.
consequences: Signature verification and event intake stay isolated from Bedrock review behavior.
Deterministic policy checks can be traced independently from AI-generated findings.
Review publication contracts remain visible as a separate integration boundary.
